preamble: Fronting of uvular nasals to alveolar nasals is a common simplification in loanword adaptation or as part of a general shift towards more forward points of articulation in the oral cavity.

examples.note: In many Japanese dialects, the uvular nasal /ɴ/ is fronted to /n/ before dental/alveolar consonants, or simplified in certain phonetic contexts.
Historical fronting of uvular nasals to alveolar in some Eastern Arctic dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
